summary: Promote 'Harness Runner 指南' to a root-level command and adapt menu ordering/persistence to support legacy 'guides' token while preserving user-configured order.
keyDecisions: Promote 'Harness Runner 指南' to a top-level command 'harness-runner.showGuide' to open the guide directly.
Preserve backward compatibility by recognizing legacy 'guides' token in persisted 'harness-runner.rootMenuOrder' and injecting a 'guides' submenu at runtime if present.
Map legacy alias tokens to canonical ordering indices so injected legacy submenu targets are ordered consistently with user configuration.
Keep localization and menu editor payloads unchanged where possible; extend ordering logic in extension.ts to handle legacy aliases.
constraintsConfirmed: Keep TypeScript strict mode and existing test suite passing
risks: Mis-detecting legacy tokens could produce duplicate menu entries if both canonical command and legacy submenu are exposed
Edge cases in user workspace settings (invalid entries) may still require normalization
followUps: Regenerate agent map knowledge catalog/overview after UI/menu behavior changes
Add a regression test specifically for legacy persisted rootMenuOrder using 'guides' token
Document backward compatibility behavior in README and changelog

architectureNotes.moduleBoundaries: Menu ordering and injection logic localized to extension.ts; localization.ts remains authoritative for menu content; menuOrderEditor handles UI payloads.
architectureNotes.responsibilityClarity: extension.ts - composition and ordering; localization.ts - menu definitions/labels; menuOrderEditor - editing and persistence UI.
architectureNotes.reuseOpportunities: Factor alias mapping and order-normalization into a small utility for reuse by other menu-related code paths.
architectureNotes.rollbackSeams: Revert by restoring previous commit; persisted workspace settings remain intact; removal of alias mapping would restore canonical-only behavior.
